You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw тАФ roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation тАФ but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` тАФ a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions тАФ The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style тАФ environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit тАФ Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` тАФ small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox тАФ Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox тАФ list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting тАФ How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session тАФ block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check тАФ Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors тАФ use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag тАФ Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) тАФ declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects тАФ via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch тАФ Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving тАФ the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git тАФ How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace тАФ root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references тАФ The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml тАФ how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents тАФ How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work тАФ spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope тАФ you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0027-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint тАФ Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end тАФ keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug тАФ Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run тАФ missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors тАФ applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor тАФ Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier тАФ keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber тАФ Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever тАФ Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] тАФ Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task тАФ starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper тАФ then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits тАФ use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` тАФ it recompiles on the next run.

- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable тАФ critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4тА▓ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try тАФ no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.

- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv тЙл X.homOfLE hdom.ge тЙл f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = тКд` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr тЯитКд, тАжтЯй` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.╬╣_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_╬╣` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg тАФ the parser rejects `(╬╣ := тАж)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict тАж).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements тАФ Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C тЛЩ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. **Caveat 1 тАФ pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/├йtale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now тАФ record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 тАФ `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free тДд/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, ├йtale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient тЯиsorryтЯй` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win тАФ Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" тАФ **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Function-field pullback of a rational map
10	
11	For a rational map `f : X тдП Y` out of an integral scheme `X`, Mathlib's
12	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField` gives a morphism
13	`Spec K(X) тЯ╢ Y` (the restriction of `f` to the generic point). Composing it with
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo` produces the pullback of germs
15	`ЁЭТк_{Y, f(╬╖_X)} тЯ╢ K(X)` at the image of the generic point of `X`.
16	
17	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, that image point is the generic
18	point of `Y`, so the germ pullback becomes a field homomorphism
19	`K(Y) тЯ╢ K(X)` тАФ the function-field functoriality `K(Y) тЖТ K(X)` that Milne's
20	rational-map extension leg (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`) and the
21	Weil-divisor obstruction (`thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`) both require.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` тАФ the germ pullback
26	  `ЁЭТк_{Y, f(╬╖_X)} тЯ╢ K(X)` (no dominance needed).
27	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` тАФ for a dominant
28	  rational map into an irreducible target, `f(╬╖_X) = ╬╖_Y`.
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback` тАФ the induced field homomorphism
30	  `K(Y) тЯ╢ K(X)` for a dominant rational map.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace IsLocalRing
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	namespace Scheme
42	
43	namespace RationalMap
44	
45	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-- **Germ pullback along a rational map.** For a rational map `f : X тдП Y` out of
48	an integral scheme `X`, the associated morphism `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) тЯ╢ Y`
49	induces, by `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo`, a local ring homomorphism from the germ
50	of `Y` at the image `f(╬╖_X)` of the generic point of `X` into the function field
51	`K(X)`. -/
52	noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
53	    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) тЯ╢ X.functionField :=
54	  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField
55	
56	/-!
57	## Dominant refinement
58	
59	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
60	`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
61	becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) тЯ╢ K(X)`.
62	-/
63	
64	/-- **For a dominant rational map into an irreducible target, the function-field
65	morphism sends the closed point of `Spec K(X)` to the generic point of `Y`.**
66	
67	Reducing to a representative partial map `g`, the morphism
68	`g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (╬╖_X) тЙл g.hom` sends the closed
69	point of `Spec ЁЭТк_{X, ╬╖_X} = Spec K(X)` to `g.hom (╬╖_{g.domain})`. The inner point
70	is the generic point of the domain (checked after applying the open immersion
71	`g.domain.╬╣`, using `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_╬╣` and `fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`),
72	and a dominant morphism sends the generic point of its (irreducible) source to the
73	generic point of `Y` (image of a generic point is generic on the dense range, and
74	generic points are unique in the TтВА space `Y`). -/
75	theorem fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
76	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
77	    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
78	  obtain тЯиg, rflтЯй := f.exists_rep
79	  haveI : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp тА╣_тА║
80	  have h╬╖ : genericPoint X тИИ g.domain :=
81	    (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec
82	  haveI : Nonempty (g.domain : Type _) := тЯитЯиgenericPoint X, h╬╖тЯйтЯй
83	  haveI : IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
84	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
85	  -- reduce the rational-map `fromFunctionField` to the partial-map one, then to the
86	  -- composite `g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (╬╖_X) h╬╖ тЙл g.hom`
87	  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
88	  have hcomp : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g
89	      = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) h╬╖ тЙл g.hom := rfl
90	  rw [hcomp, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
91	  -- the inner point is the generic point of the domain
92	  have hp : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) h╬╖) (closedPoint X.functionField)
93	      = genericPoint g.domain.toScheme := by
94	    apply (Scheme.Opens.╬╣ g.domain).isOpenEmbedding.injective
95	    rw [genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.╬╣ g.domain),
96	      тЖР Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_╬╣]
97	    exact Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint
98	  rw [hp]
99	  -- a dominant morphism sends the generic point of an irreducible source to `genericPoint Y`
100	  have himg := (genericPoint_spec g.domain.toScheme).image g.hom.continuous
101	  rw [Set.image_univ, g.hom.denseRange.closure_range] at himg
102	  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)
103	
104	/-- **The induced field homomorphism `K(Y) тЯ╢ K(X)` of a dominant rational map.**
105	
106	Since `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`
107	(`fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`), the germ pullback `stalkPullback` has
108	source `ЁЭТк_{Y, ╬╖_Y} = K(Y)` up to the canonical stalk isomorphism, giving the
109	function-field functoriality `K(Y) тЖТ K(X)` required by the rational-map extension
110	leg of Milne's Albanese argument. -/
111	noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
112	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
113	    Y.functionField тЯ╢ X.functionField :=
114	  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr
115	    (Inseparable.of_eq (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom тЙл f.stalkPullback
116	
117	end RationalMap
118	
119	end Scheme
120	
121	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib

/-!
# Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism

Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides only `compHom`,
the composition of a rational map with a *morphism on the right*
(`f : X тдП Y`, `g : Y тЯ╢ Z` тЖж `X тдП Z`). The dual operation тАФ precomposing a
rational map with a morphism *on the left* тАФ is missing.

For a morphism `p : W тЯ╢ X` whose underlying continuous map is **open**, and a
rational map `f : X тдП Y`, the composite `f тИШ p : W тдП Y` is a well-defined
rational map. Openness of `p` is exactly what guarantees that the preimage
`pтБ╗┬╣(f.domain)` of the (dense open) domain of definition stays dense
(`Dense.preimage`), so that the pulled-back partial map is again a partial map,
and that the construction descends to equivalence classes.

This is the first missing brick in Milne's difference-map construction
`╬ж = m тИШ (f ├Ч f) : X ├Ч X тдП G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3): the two
projections `prс╡в : X ├Ч X тЯ╢ X` are open (flat, locally of finite presentation,
being base changes of the smooth structure morphism `X тЯ╢ Spec k╠Д`), so
`f.precomp prс╡в` is the pullback of `f` along a projection.

## Main definitions

* `Scheme.PartialMap.precomp` тАФ precomposition of a partial map with an open
  morphism.
* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` тАФ precomposition of a rational map with an open
  morphism, descended from `PartialMap.precomp`.

## Main results

* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp_compHom` тАФ precomposition commutes with
  right-composition, `(f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp`.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

variable {W X Y Z : Scheme.{u}}

namespace PartialMap

/-- **Auxiliary restriction square.** For a morphism `p : W тЯ╢ X` and opens
`V тЙд U` of `X`, restricting `p тИг_ U` to the smaller preimage `p тБ╗┬╣с╡Б V` agrees
with `p тИг_ V` followed by the inclusion `V тЖк U`. Both sides are pinned down by
composing with the mono `U.╬╣`. -/
lemma homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict (p : W тЯ╢ X) {U V : X.Opens} (hUV : V тЙд U)
    (h : p тБ╗┬╣с╡Б V тЙд p тБ╗┬╣с╡Б U) :
    W.homOfLE h тЙл (p тИг_ U) = (p тИг_ V) тЙл X.homOfLE hUV := by
  rw [тЖР cancel_mono U.╬╣, Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_╬╣,
    тЖР Category.assoc (W.homOfLE h), Scheme.homOfLE_╬╣, Category.assoc,
    Scheme.homOfLE_╬╣, morphismRestrict_╬╣]

/-- **Precomposition of a partial map with an open morphism.** For `f : X тдП Y`
(as a partial map) and a morphism `p : W тЯ╢ X` whose underlying map is open, the
composite `f тИШ p : W тдП Y` is defined on `pтБ╗┬╣(f.domain)`, which is dense because
open maps pull dense sets back to dense sets. -/
@[simps domain hom]
noncomputable def precomp (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W тЯ╢ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
    W.PartialMap Y where
  domain := p тБ╗┬╣с╡Б f.domain
  dense_domain := f.dense_domain.preimage hp
  hom := (p тИг_ f.domain) тЙл f.hom

/-- Precomposition respects the equivalence of partial maps, hence descends to
rational maps. -/
lemma precomp_equiv {f g : X.PartialMap Y} (p : W тЯ╢ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
    (h : f.equiv g) : (f.precomp p hp).equiv (g.precomp p hp) := by

/-- **Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism.** For `f : X тдП Y`
and a morphism `p : W тЯ╢ X` whose underlying map is open, `f.precomp p hp : W тдП Y`
is the composite `f тИШ p`, obtained by pulling any representative partial map back

/-- Precomposition on the left commutes with right-composition by a morphism.
This is what lets Milne's difference map be assembled as
